Name: Scyther
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike.
Flavor-Text: The sharp scythes on its forearms become increasingly sharp by cutting through hard objects.

Name: Pineco
Flavor-Text: It spits out a fluid that it uses to glue tree bark to its body. The fluid hardens when it touches air.

Name: Seedot
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: If it remains still, it looks just like a real nut. It delights in surprising foraging Pokémon.

Name: Nuzleaf
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

Name: Shiftry
Effects: Choose 3 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. If you do, shuffle all of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that you didn’t choose, and all cards attached to those Pokémon, into their deck.
Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe’s mind and take preemptive action.

Name: Shroomish
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: If it senses danger, it scatters spores from the top of its head to protect itself.

Name: Breloom
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: The seeds on its tail are made of toxic spores. It knocks out foes with quick, virtually invisible punches.

Name: Roselia
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its flowers give off a relaxing fragrance. The stronger its aroma, the healthier the Roselia is.

Name: Roserade
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

Name: Turtwig
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Photosynthesis occurs across its body under the sun. The shell on its back is actually hardened soil.

Name: Grotle
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It lives along water in forests. In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.

Name: Torterra ex
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your [G] Pokémon in play.
Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shaymin
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon ex or Benched Pokémon V. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

Name: Cottonee
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

Name: Whimsicott
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal all damage from your Active [G] Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, discard all Energy from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it’ll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.

Name: Deerling
Effects: Discard a [G] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Deerling have different scents depending on the season. In this form, Deerling have a softly sweet scent that lingers in the nose.

Name: Sawsbuck
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Sawsbuck in their Autumn Form are known for having very short tempers. The males fight each other incessantly.

Name: Grubbin
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Grubbin and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its natural enemies, like Rookidee, may flee rather than risk getting caught in its large mandibles that can snap thick tree branches.

Name: Dhelmise
Effects: If you have any [M] Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

Name: Bramblin
Flavor-Text: Not even Bramblin knows where it is headed as it tumbles across the wilderness, blown by the wind. It loathes getting wet.

Name: Brambleghast
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon gets +50 HP for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Brambleghast wanders around arid regions. On rare occasions, mass outbreaks of these Pokémon will bury an entire town.

Name: Scovillain ex
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand. Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rellor
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It rolls its mud ball around while the energy it needs for evolution matures. Eventually the time comes for it to evolve.

Name: Rabsca
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to your Benched Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An infant sleeps inside the ball. Rabsca rolls the ball soothingly with its legs to ensure the infant sleeps comfortably.

Name: Iron Leaves ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, you may move any amount of Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ponyta
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It can’t run properly when it’s newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

Name: Slugma
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Burned, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

Name: Magcargo
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent’s Active Pokémon moves to the Bench during their turn, their new Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

Name: Victini
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
Discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each tails, discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There’s a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill.

Name: Litten
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It spends even the smallest amount of downtime grooming its fur with its tongue. Loose fur gathers in its stomach and serves as fuel for fiery moves.

Name: Torracat
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Flare Strike.
Flavor-Text: In the midst of battle, the fire pouch on Torracat’s neck rings like a bell and produces stronger flames than usual.

Name: Incineroar ex
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by this Pokémon cost [C] less for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Turtonator
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

Name: Sizzlipede
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

Name: Centiskorch
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon isn’t Burned, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

Name: Gouging Fire ex
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Blaze Blitz again until it leaves the Active Spot.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Totodile
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: It is small but rough and tough. It won’t hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

Name: Croconaw
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

Name: Feraligatr
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 5 damage counters on this Pokémon. If you do, during this turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Giant Wave.
Flavor-Text: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

Name: Carvanha
Flavor-Text: It won’t attack while it’s alone—not even if it spots prey. Instead, it waits for other Carvanha to join it, and then the Pokémon attack as a group.

Name: Sharpedo
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: As soon as it catches the scent of prey, Sharpedo will jet seawater from its backside, hurtling toward the target to attack at 75 mph.

Name: Keldeo
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: Keldeo has strengthened its resolve for battle, filling its body with power and changing its form.

Name: Snom
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It eats snow that has accumulated on the ground. It prefers soft, freshly fallen snow, so it will eat its way up a mountain, aiming for the peak.

Name: Frosmoth
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Frosmoth senses air currents with its antennae. It sends its scales drifting on frigid air, making them fall like snow.

Name: Wiglett
Effects: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

Name: Finizen
Flavor-Text: It likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.

Name: Palafin
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself for each damage counter on it.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s ancient genes have awakened. It is now so extraordinarily strong that it can easily lift a cruise ship with one fin.

Name: Walking Wake ex
Ability-Effect: Damage from attacks used by this Pokémon isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

Name: Raichu
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to each Pokémon that has any damage counters on it (both yours and your opponent’s), except for this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.

Name: Electabuzz
Flavor-Text: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

Name: Electivire
Effects: If your opponent has any [W] Pokémon in play, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

Name: Charjabug
Flavor-Text: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

Name: Vikavolt
Effects: This attack does 80 more damage for each of your Benched Charjabug.
Flavor-Text: It builds up electricity in its abdomen, focuses it through its jaws, and then fires the electricity off in concentrated beams.

Name: Zeraora
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It runs as fast as lightning strikes, shredding its opponents with its high-voltage claws.

Name: Yamper
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

Name: Boltund
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [L] Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

Name: Wugtrio ex
Effects: Discard up to 3 Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each Energy card you discarded in this way. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Iron Hands
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon shares many similarities with Iron Hands, an object mentioned in a certain expedition journal.

Name: Iron Thorns
Effects: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 70 damage for each Future card you find there. Then, discard those Future cards and shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Some of its notable features match those of an object named within a certain expedition journal as Iron Thorns.

Name: Mr. Mime
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. You may use the effect of a Supporter card you find there as the effect of this attack.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.

Name: Marill
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The fur on its body naturally repels water. It can stay dry even when it plays in the water.

Name: Azumarill
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of things in water and tell what they are.

Name: Girafarig
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Girafarig’s tail has a small head. It instinctively bites at any foe that approaches the Pokémon from behind.

Name: Latias
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: It communicates using telepathy. Its body is covered in down that refracts light to make it invisible.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [P] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor’s back contained a mysterious power.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

Name: Solosis
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It communicates with others telepathically. Its body is encapsulated in liquid, but if it takes a heavy blow, the liquid will leak out.

Name: Duosion
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its psychic power can supposedly cover a range of more than half a mile—but only if its two brains can agree with each other.

Name: Reuniclus
Effects: Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. You may put any number of Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: While it could use its psychic abilities in battle, this Pokémon prefers to swing its powerful arms around to beat opponents into submission.

Name: Elgyem
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If this Pokémon stands near a TV, strange scenery will appear on the screen. That scenery is said to be from its home.

Name: Beheeyem
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play.
Flavor-Text: Whenever a Beheeyem visits a farm, a Dubwool mysteriously disappears.

Name: Cutiefly
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Nectar and pollen are its favorite fare. You can find Cutiefly hovering around Gossifleur, trying to get some of Gossifleur’s pollen.

Name: Ribombee
Effects: During your next turn, if the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out, take 2 more Prize cards.
Flavor-Text: It makes pollen puffs from pollen and nectar. The puffs’ effects depend on the type of ingredients and how much of each one is used.

Name: Scream Tail
Effects: Heal 100 damage from 1 of your Benched Ancient Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.

Name: Flutter Mane
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Active Pokémon has no Abilities, except for Midnight Fluttering.
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.

Name: Iron Valiant
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Zen Blade.
Flavor-Text: It’s possible that this is the object listed as Iron Valiant in a certain expedition journal.

Name: Iron Valiant
Effects: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.
If you played a Future Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It’s possible that this is the object listed as Iron Valiant in a certain expedition journal.

Name: Iron Crown ex
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your Future Pokémon, except any Iron Crown ex, do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on those Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Meditite
Flavor-Text: It meditates to heighten its inner energy and to float in the air. It eats one berry a day.

Name: Medicham
Flavor-Text: It elegantly avoids attacks with dance-like steps, then launches a devastating blow in the same motion.

Name: Relicanth
Ability-Effect: Each of your evolved Pokémon can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Flavor-Text: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

Name: Drilbur
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 Basic [F] Energy cards and discard them. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It brings its claws together and whirls around at high speed before rushing toward its prey.

Name: Excadrill
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It’s not uncommon for tunnels that appear to have formed naturally to actually be a result of Excadrill’s rampant digging.

Name: Golett
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

Name: Golurk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams.

Name: Rockruff
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is very friendly when it’s young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

Name: Lycanroc
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic [F] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: Lycanroc attacks its prey with its sharp claws and fangs. It loyally obeys the instructions of a Trainer it trusts.

Name: Mudbray
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon covers itself in mud that it has regurgitated. The mud won’t dry out even if it’s exposed to the sun for a long time.

Name: Mudsdale
Effects: Attach a Basic [F] Energy card from your discard pile to each of your Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has been treasured not just for its physical labor but also because it produces high-quality mud used for making pottery.

Name: Rolycoly
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, Rolycoly will come out onto grassy plains and roll around. Then it will eat the grass that gets tangled in its wheel-like leg.

Name: Carkol
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The temperature inside its body increases when it experiences strong emotions. It rolls around frantically while spewing flames.

Name: Coalossal
Effects: This attack does 80 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: To intimidate its opponents, Coalossal will vigorously shake its body, scattering coal from its smoldering back.

Name: Great Tusk
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.

Name: Great Tusk
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck. If you played an Ancient Supporter card from your hand during this turn, discard 3 more cards in this way.
Flavor-Text: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.

Name: Sandy Shocks
Effects: If you have 3 or more Energy in play, this attack does 70 more damage. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness.
Flavor-Text: No records exist of this Pokémon being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon’s traits match up with a creature shown in an expedition journal.

Name: Iron Boulder ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ekans
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The older it gets, the longer it grows. At night, it wraps its long body around tree branches to rest.

Name: Arbok
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The pattern on its belly appears to be a frightening face. Weak foes will flee just at the sight of the pattern.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

Name: Haunter
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

Name: Gengar ex
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Effects: You may move an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Poochyena
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.

Name: Mightyena
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.

Name: Sableye
Effects: You may move any number of damage counters from your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to their Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

Name: Farigiraf ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Basic Pokémon ex.
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Roaring Moon
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each Ancient card in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: It is possible that this is the creature listed as Roaring Moon in an expedition journal that still holds many mysteries.

Name: Forretress
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

Name: Scizor ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard up to 2 [M] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

Name: Beldum
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: From its rear, Beldum emits a magnetic force that rapidly pulls opponents in. They get skewered on Beldum’s sharp claws.

Name: Metang
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 4 cards of your deck and attach any number of Basic [M] Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards and put them on the bottom of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Two Beldum have become stuck together via their own magnetic forces. With two brains, the resulting Metang has doubled psychic powers.

Name: Metagross
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Meteor Mash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Because the magnetic powers of these Pokémon get stronger in freezing temperatures, Metagross living on snowy mountains are full of energy.

Name: Meltan
Flavor-Text: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

Name: Melmetal
Flavor-Text: At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.

Name: Iron Treads
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon has a Future Booster Energy Capsule attached, it is [F] and [M] type.
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. It resembles a mysterious object described in an old expedition journal.

Name: Koraidon
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Ancient Pokémon in play.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

Name: Koraidon ex
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Miraidon
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Future Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

Name: Miraidon ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Cyber Drive.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Raging Bolt ex
Effects: Discard your hand and draw 6 cards.
You may discard any amount of Basic Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lickitung
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Put up to 2 Basic Pokémon you find there onto your opponent’s Bench.
Flavor-Text: If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.

Name: Lickilicky
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Lickilicky’s strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body. No one has figured out how Lickilicky’s tongue can stretch so far.

Name: Hoothoot
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flavor-Text: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

Name: Noctowl
Effects: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It creates mazes in dark locations. When spotted, it flees into the ground by digging with its tail.

Name: Dudunsparce
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses its hard tail to make its nest by boring holes into bedrock deep underground. The nest can reach lengths of over six miles.

Name: Skitty
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It can’t stop itself from chasing moving things, and it runs in a circle, chasing its own tail.

Name: Delcatty
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
You may move any amount of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It dislikes dirty places. It often searches for a comfortable place in which to groom itself.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it’s one of them. This way they won’t attack it.

Name: Pidove
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon’s remaining HP is 30 or less, you may search your deck for an Unfezant or Unfezant ex and put it onto this Pidove to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Where people go, these Pokémon follow. If you’re scattering food for them, be careful— several hundred of them can gather at once.

Name: Tranquill
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It can fly moderately quickly. No matter how far it travels, it can always find its way back to its master and its nest.

Name: Unfezant
Effects: You may put 2 Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon into their hand.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Unfezant are exceptional fliers. The females are known for their stamina, while the males outclass them in terms of speed.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Discard up to 2 Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

Name: Cinccino
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each Special Energy card attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won’t tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

Name: Drampa
Effects: If all of your Benched Pokémon have at least 1 damage counter on them, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

Name: Iron Jugulis
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s possible that Iron Jugulis, an object described in an old book, may actually be this Pokémon.

Name: Ancient Booster Energy Capsule
Description: The Ancient Pokémon this card is attached to gets +60 HP, recovers from all Special Conditions, and can’t be affected by any Special Conditions.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Awakening Drum
Description: Draw a card for each of your Ancient Pokémon in play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Bianca’s Devotion
Description: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has 30 HP or less remaining.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Boxed Order
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Buddy-Buddy Poffin
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Ciphermaniac’s Codebreaking
Description: Search your deck for 2 cards, shuffle your deck, then put those cards on top of it in any order.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Eri
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you discard up to 2 Item cards you find there.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Explorer’s Guidance
Description: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Discard the other cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Full Metal Lab
Description: [M] Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) take 30 less damage from attacks from the opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Future Booster Energy Capsule
Description: The Future Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost, and the attacks it uses do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Hand Trimmer
Description: Each player discards cards from their hand until they have 5 cards in their hand. Your opponent discards first. (If a player has 5 or fewer cards in their hand, they do not discard.)
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Heavy Baton
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has a Retreat Cost of exactly 4, is in the Active Spot, and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, move up to 3 Basic Energy cards from that Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Hero’s Cape
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to gets +100 HP.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Master Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Maximum Belt
Description: Attacks used by the Pokémon this card is attached to do 50 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Morty’s Conviction
Description: You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.Draw a card for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Perilous Jungle
Description: During Pokémon Checkup, put 2 more damage counters on each Poisoned non-[D] Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Prime Catcher
Description: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. If you do, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Reboot Pod
Description: Attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to each of your Future Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Rescue Board
Description: The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is [C] less. If that Pokémon’s remaining HP is 30 or less, it has no Retreat Cost.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Salvatore
Description: Search your deck for a card that has no Abilities and evolves from 1 of your Pokémon, and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. You can use this card on a Pokémon you put down when you were setting up to play or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Mist Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to the Pokémon this card is attached to. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)

Name: Neo Upper Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.If this card is attached to a Stage 2 Pokémon, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 2 Energy at a time.

Name: Shiftry
Effects: Choose 3 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. If you do, shuffle all of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that you didn’t choose, and all cards attached to those Pokémon, into their deck.
Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that was feared as a forest guardian. It can read the foe’s mind and take preemptive action.

Name: Grotle
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It lives along water in forests. In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.

Name: Deerling
Effects: Discard a [G] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Deerling have different scents depending on the season. In this form, Deerling have a softly sweet scent that lingers in the nose.

Name: Sawsbuck
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Sawsbuck in their Autumn Form are known for having very short tempers. The males fight each other incessantly.

Name: Litten
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It spends even the smallest amount of downtime grooming its fur with its tongue. Loose fur gathers in its stomach and serves as fuel for fiery moves.

Name: Snom
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It eats snow that has accumulated on the ground. It prefers soft, freshly fallen snow, so it will eat its way up a mountain, aiming for the peak.

Name: Charjabug
Flavor-Text: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [P] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor’s back contained a mysterious power.

Name: Reuniclus
Effects: Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. You may put any number of Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: While it could use its psychic abilities in battle, this Pokémon prefers to swing its powerful arms around to beat opponents into submission.

Name: Cutiefly
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Nectar and pollen are its favorite fare. You can find Cutiefly hovering around Gossifleur, trying to get some of Gossifleur’s pollen.

Name: Relicanth
Ability-Effect: Each of your evolved Pokémon can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Flavor-Text: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

Name: Excadrill
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It’s not uncommon for tunnels that appear to have formed naturally to actually be a result of Excadrill’s rampant digging.

Name: Mudsdale
Effects: Attach a Basic [F] Energy card from your discard pile to each of your Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has been treasured not just for its physical labor but also because it produces high-quality mud used for making pottery.

Name: Arbok
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The pattern on its belly appears to be a frightening face. Weak foes will flee just at the sight of the pattern.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

Name: Metagross
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Meteor Mash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Because the magnetic powers of these Pokémon get stronger in freezing temperatures, Metagross living on snowy mountains are full of energy.

Name: Meltan
Flavor-Text: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

Name: Lickitung
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Put up to 2 Basic Pokémon you find there onto your opponent’s Bench.
Flavor-Text: If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it’s one of them. This way they won’t attack it.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Discard up to 2 Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

Name: Cinccino
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each Special Energy card attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won’t tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

Name: Drampa
Effects: If all of your Benched Pokémon have at least 1 damage counter on them, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.

Name: Torterra ex
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your [G] Pokémon in play.
Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Iron Leaves ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, you may move any amount of Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Incineroar ex
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by this Pokémon cost [C] less for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gouging Fire ex
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Blaze Blitz again until it leaves the Active Spot.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Walking Wake ex
Ability-Effect: Damage from attacks used by this Pokémon isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Wugtrio ex
Effects: Discard up to 3 Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each Energy card you discarded in this way. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Iron Crown ex
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your Future Pokémon, except any Iron Crown ex, do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on those Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Iron Boulder ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gengar ex
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Effects: You may move an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Farigiraf ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Basic Pokémon ex.
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Scizor ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard up to 2 [M] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
